DataListItem.Notes: [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x29 -> RAM 0xf970]

This value is calculated in EvoScan, ensure you select Injector Pulse Width if you want to get this Duty Cycle percentage. Ensure IPW is a checked item in the list above this item, if you have the Injector Pulse Width after the IDC, it wont log IDC, they have to be in the correct order.
Injector duty cycle % , which is RPM * 'Injector ms' / 1200. If you are regularly seeing over 90% duty, you may need bigger injectors. The injectors must have enough 'head room' too cope with unexpectedly high air flows - these may be caused by overboost, faults and particularly cold weather. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x21 -> RAM 0xf17b]

Displays state of the throttle. High voltage or % value means a more open throttle. When logging engine activity (especially on the dyno), it is useful to log throttle position. This makes it easy to see when a power run begins and ends i.e. when the driver's foot is depressing the accelerator fully. Some mitsubishis show up to 20% when your foot is off the accelerator, and will show a max of 90-99% when your foot is fully applied, this is normal. In Spec: 0.535 − 0.735 V would translate as 0.535/5.0V= 10.7 to 14.7 at Idle, if its not then unscrew and move your tps until it does. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x17 -> RAM 0xf13d]
No Response from Vehicle ECU [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x21 -> RAM 0xf17b]
[RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x06 -> RAM 0xf361]

Long Term Fuel Trim - Low, now represented in terms of a percentage. make sure you are within the 5% band, because outside of 5% you should rescale your injectors.
Old Formula: 0.78125*x [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x0C -> RAM 0xee99]

Long Term Fuel Trim - Mid make sure you are within the 5% band, because outside of 5% you should rescale your injectors.
Old Formula: 0.78125*x [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x0D -> RAM 0xee9b]

Long Term Fuel Trim - High make sure you are within the 5% band, because outside of 5% you should rescale your injectors.
Old Formula: 0.78125*x [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x0E -> RAM 0xee9d]

Long Term Fuel Trim - High make sure you are within the 5% band, because outside of 5% you should rescale your injectors.
Old Formula: 0.78125*x [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x50 -> RAM 0xf27f]

Short Term Fuel Trim - When the fuelling is under closed loop control by the lambda sensor(s), this refers to the amount of fuel added or subtracted from the value retrieved from the fuel map. -5% would mean that the ecu is fuelling 5% less than the map says in order to achieve the ideal air/fuel ratio. Under high loads, the ECU switches off closed loop control, and uses values from the map. At this point, you will see AFC drop to 0%. This is why it is important that fuelling mapping is accurate (or at least rich) at high loads - the ECU does not compensate for errors here. Old Formula: 0.78125*x [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x0F -> RAM 0xf26e]
No Response from Vehicle ECU [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x07 -> RAM 0xf109]

Measured voltage from the car battery. May be as low as 10 Volts when ignition is off. Should rise to around 14-15 Volts when the engine is running. If you see less than 12 volts when the engine is running, then you will need to get your Alternator looked at. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x14 -> RAM 0xf13b]

Not available on all Mitsubishis, generally only those from 1998 onwards allow the speed to be logged from the EFI ecu, see the ABS ecu if you wish to log vehicle speed on earlier vehicles. For UK Vehicles, select 'U.K. Vehicle speed correction' from the Logging menu above, this is because the UK mitsubishis use a US ecu with a piggyback speed converter. So it applies a further calculation to bring it back to MPH/KPH as required.
Speed of wheel rotation in kilometres per hour/miles per hour for standard wheel circumference. Data value may not be updated as frequently as engine speed(RPM), hence acceleration times may be more accurately determined from engine speed(RPM). This value may not be accurate if the car wheels or gears have been changed from standard, since this changes the rolling circumference.
For UK Vehicles, use the UK Logging option under the Logging menu, or use these functions: KPH = 3.218688*x MPH = 2*x
[RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x2F -> RAM 0xf1e9]

Load 1 Byte (Load1B) is only available if you are running a Tephra Modified ecu rom. Check the tuning guide on www.evoscan.com on how to do this. This Load value is calculated by the ecu internally, and is based on AirFlow (MAF) Sensor and Crankshaft Position Sensor (RPM), i.e. more air flow and less crankshaft speed will give you higher Load values. i.e. going up a steep hill with your foot to the floor. Load is effectively similiar to this calculation: (current airflow / peak airflow) * (Baro And AirTemp Compensation) * 100%.
Load1B doesnt use the injector scaling value on the main evoscan screen, this value is already available internally to the ecu code. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x41 -> RAM 0xeed9]

Knock Sum counter from the knock sensor, accurate for 1998+ Mitsubishi vehicles. use KnockSum3E for older mitsubishi knock sum readings. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x26 -> RAM 0xf3a3]

This value is great for 1998 to 2007 Mitsubishi vehicles. For pre1997 mitsi use Coolant Temp. Temperature of the radiator coolant water. Expect to see around 85degC - 95degC from a warmed up engine. Don't work the engine too hard until the temperature is at least 80 degrees. During racetrack use you will regularly see up to 110-115DegC. If the coolant temp goes this high, ensure you leave the key on, but not running, for 5 minutes after you have stopped to keep the fan flowing over the radiator and engine block. Anything over 120degC is going to be of a major concern, and can indicate a radiator water leak, a water pump leak, or a blown headgasket. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x10 -> RAM 0xf10f]

[RPM]*[InjPulseWidth]*20*6/[Speed]/1200
NaN = Not a Number. i.e. when Speed is 0, then the calculation will divide by zero and will display NaN. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x21 -> RAM 0xf17b]

Manifold Absolute & Relative Pressure This is boost pressure, and may be represented as absolute or relative, depending on the ECU - some ECUs report both parameters, whilst some only report one. Absolute pressure in the manifold is relative to a vacuum. Subtract approx 14.5 PSI to get relative pressure. When boost pressure in the manifold is shown as relative to atmospheric pressure, negative values represent partial vacuums in the manifold. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x38 -> RAM 0xf15f]

For 1998+ Mitsubishi, use MAF Air Temp Scaled. Temperature of air drawn into the engine for combustion. Generally measured at the point of entry to the air filter. This will not give an indication of charge temperature. However intake temperature is useful to the ECU for determination of the wastegate duty cycle required to produce a given boost pressure. High boost pressures may be attained with lower wastegate duty cycles when the IAT is low.
[RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x3A -> RAM 0xf117]

This value is great for 1998 to 2007 Mitsubishi vehicles. For pre1997 mitsi use Air Temperature. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x11 -> RAM 0xf119]

At sea level, this should be around 100kpa. On some vehicles, the value is not updated continuously, since a single pressure sensor is shared for reading both manifold and atmospheric pressures, a solenoid being using to switch the input to the sensor. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x15 -> RAM 0xf12f]

The rate of flow of air into the engine. Some ECUs report air flow voltage, whilst others report a calculated flow rate. The voltage from which the ecu calculates mass air flow is non-linear, with smaller changes in output voltage being seen for flow changes at high rates compared with low flow rates. It is from mass air flow that the ECU calculates engine load which has a big influence on ignition timing and fuelling.
[RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x1A -> RAM 0xf1df]
[RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x1D -> RAM 0xf18d]

These parameters report the output voltage of the O2 sensors. Early vehicles tend to have a single 'Front O2 Sensor', whereas newer vehicles have both a 'Front A/F Sensor' and a 'Rear O2 Sensor'. These sensors do not report an accurate air/fuel ratio, but instead provide a rich/lean signal to the ECU. Their output voltages switches sharply as the AFR crosses the stoichiometric ratio. Values of approx 0 to 0.9 Volts are normal. 0 being lean, 0.9 being very rich. The sensor voltage will oscillate between these extremes when under closed loop control. Under high loads, the voltage should never drop below 0.7 Volts. If it does, this means that the fuel mixture is too lean when on boost. Quite possibly there is a fault with the air flow sensor.
The addition of cone style induction kits, whilst improving top end power and throttle response is known to upset air/fuel ratios. Alteration of the ecu calibration (AKA a remap) is the solution. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x13 -> RAM 0xf11f]

When the vehicle is at Idle, this is the value the ECU will try and target. It will show higher values at colder tempereratures i.e 1250rpm, and 750 after the car has warmed up. Generally the ECU adds 50-150rpm to the Target RPM when the Air Conditioner is turned on to counter-act the slight extra load in the engine from the a/c compressor. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x24 -> RAM 0xf401]

These parameters report the output voltage of the O2 sensors. Early vehicles tend to have a single 'Front O2 Sensor', whereas newer vehicles have both a 'Front A/F Sensor' and a 'Rear O2 Sensor'. These sensors do not report an accurate air/fuel ratio, but instead provide a rich/lean signal to the ECU. Their output voltages switches sharply as the AFR crosses the stoichiometric ratio. Values of approx 0 to 0.9 Volts are normal. 0 being lean, 0.9 being very rich. The sensor voltage will oscillate between these extremes when under closed loop control. Under high loads, the voltage should never drop below 0.7 Volts. If it does, this means that the fuel mixture is too lean when on boost. Quite possibly there is a fault with the air flow sensor.
The addition of cone style induction kits, whilst improving top end power and throttle response is known to upset air/fuel ratios. Alteration of the ecu calibration (AKA a remap) is the solution. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x3C -> RAM 0xf123]

RPM/Speed=Gear
Using this value you should be able to tell which gear it is for your vehicle, it is a raw RPM divided by Speed value because each vehicle can have different gear ratios.
if you get NaN, this means speed is Zero, and you can't divide by zero. NaN stands for Not a Number.
[RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x21 -> RAM 0xf17b]

This is an internal Mitsubishi ECU calculated value that determines how much air volume is used in the cylinders. It is based primarily calculated from the MAF Sensor (Mass Airflow Intake Sensor) and RPM [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x2C -> RAM 0xf973]

Japan domestic market import mitsubishi vehicles Manifold Absolute & Relative Pressure. This is boost pressure, and may be represented as absolute or relative, depending on the ECU - some ECUs report both parameters, whilst some only report one. Absolute pressure in the manifold is relative to a vacuum. Subtract approx 14.5 PSI to get relative pressure. When boost pressure in the manifold is shown as relative to atmospheric pressure, negative values represent partial vacuums in the manifold.
MAP sensors do not know the local atmospheric pressure. To log boost in psig (what all boost gauges show and what people always use to discuss boost values), the conversion formula for the logging program needs to have an offset for your local atmospheric pressure. The general formula for the JDM Evo 9 MAP sensor is:
0.19347*x-b
where b is the local atmospheric pressure. The local atmospheric pressure is affected strongly affected by altitude. For example the pressure at sea level is 14.7 psi, but at 5000 ft, the pressure is 12.2 psi. That's a pretty big difference when you want to log boost. Use this table of altitude vs pressure to determine the offset pressure for your formula:
altitude (ft) local atmospheric pressure (psi) 0 14.7 500 14.4 1000 14.2 1500 13.9 2000 13.7 2500 13.4 3000 13.2 3500 12.9 4000 12.7 4500 12.5 5000 12.2 6000 11.8 7000 11.3 8000 10.9
*For example*, if you are living at 750 ft, then you'd want to use 14.3 psi as your offset, and the formula would be
0.19347*x-14.3 [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x38 -> RAM 0xf15f]

This is the Knock Sum reading used on 1995-1997 Mitsubishi. It uses the 3E command to get the MUT reading. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x3E -> RAM 0xf121]

Idle Stepper Controller used for letting air past your air intake throttle valve so that the engine can idle whilst your foot is off the accelerator. sometimes known as ISV. See the Evo manual on the evoscan.com website how to tune your idle for better off throttle performance. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x16 -> RAM 0xeed5]
[RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x4A -> RAM 0xf45d]
[RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x4A -> RAM 0xf45d]
[RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x4A -> RAM 0xf45d]
[RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x4A -> RAM 0xf45d]
[RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x49 -> RAM 0xeee9]
[RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x4A -> RAM 0xf45d]

This is the actual target AFR value used by the ECU. It is shown in the ECU Rom table settings map called High and Low Octane Fuel that contains AFRMAP values by Load and RPM. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x32 -> RAM 0xf2a5]

This is just an extended calculation to formulate the real Load value beyond that shown in ECULoad. A more accurate method is to reflash your ecu with a ROM mod that allows you to log Load1Byte (Load1B)
For Load calculated dont forget to update the InjectorScaling on the Main Form, with your injector scaling value used for your rom.
5*InjScaling*([InjPulseWidth]-(-0.1026*[Battery]+1.8741))/[AFRMAP] [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x29 -> RAM 0xf970]

just another load reading, not really used. Load1B is the most useful and only load reading required for tuning. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x1F -> RAM 0xf189]

clips at 160 due to vehicle ecu internal calculation (5*255/8) and you can only fit the numbers 0 to 255 in 1 byte being returned from the ecu to evoscan during datalogging. Best to install a Tephramod rom, and utilise Load1B instead. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x1C -> RAM 0xf187]

Total Latency is Injector_Latency_Base*Inj_batt_volt_latecy_compensat(v) /4 [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x79 -> RAM 0xf2cf]

Depreciated now, use Load1Byte instead, it has faster updates. Load MUT 2Byte will only work if you have reflashed tephra mod rom to your Evo ECU via EcuFlash and an EvoScan Openport 1.3U USB Cable. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x00 -> RAM 0xf0f6, RequestID2 0x01 -> RAM 0xf0f7]

Depreciated now, (use RPM instead, it has faster updates). RPM MUT 2Byte will only work if you have reflashed tephra mod rom to your Evo ECU via EcuFlash and an EvoScan Openport 1.3U USB Cable. This value will allow display of rpms over 8,000rpm (31.25*255) [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x02 -> RAM 0xf0fd, RequestID2 0x03 -> RAM 0xf0fc]

Airflow is good to see the MAF sensor activity as calculated by the ECU by RPM. MUT 2Byte Mod is a reflashed modification to the ECU via EcuFlash and an EvoScan Reprogramming USB Cable. This value will allow display of AirFlow over 1600hz (6.25*255) [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x04 -> RAM 0xf343, RequestID2 0x05 -> RAM 0xf363 -- WARNING: addresses not contiguous, hi/lo byte pairing UNCONFIRMED, verify against EvoScan Eval formula before trusting stitched value]

The Octane Level will start at 100% and decrease if there is persistant knock sum present. the Octane Level % is used as part of adjustment to the Timing to help protect the engine from excessive knock, normally caused by lean fuel conditions (excessive heat), or bad octane fuel. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x27 -> RAM 0xeec9] [MECHANISM CONFIRMED 2026-07, session 4: this ECU has no separate knock-retard-degrees value - Octane Level IS the mechanism. Full traced chain: knock_process_main (0x2933b) writes knock sum to RAM 0xf3a2/f3a3 -> knock_event_counter_update_eec6 (0x251c4) applies hysteresis vs ROM thresholds 0xb3e/0xb40 to drive an intermediate counter at RAM 0xeec6 (0-255, rises when clean, falls under sustained knock) -> octane_level_compute_from_knock_counter (0x252d3) clamps/scales eec6 and writes the result to eec8/eec9 (this item). A second writer, octane_level_init_or_force_from_gear_table (0x24e80), can also set this value directly from a per-gear ROM table or force it to 100% under certain conditions, independent of the knock decay path. See logging.txt session 3/4 for full detail.]
Exhaust Gas Recirculation Temperature. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x12 -> RAM 0xf111]

Manifold Air Temperature Scaled. This measures the temperature at the intake after the turbo but just before the air enters the engine. The lower the temperature the better for performance. A larger or more efficient intercooler will bring your manifold air temperatures down more. Also EGT, (Exhaust Gas Temps) is another good sensor to monitor. EGT is not a standard sensor on the Mitsubishi vehicles though. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x44 -> RAM 0xeedf]

Waste Gate Duty Cycle. Used by the ecu for boost control for those 1998+ vehicles that have a efi ecu controlled boost control solenoid. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x86 -> RAM 0xf44d]
[RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x8A -> RAM 0xf13f]

Waste Gate Duty Cycle. Used by the ecu to adjust boost control for those 1998+ vehicles that have a efi ecu controlled boost control solenoid. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x8B -> RAM 0xf4ad]

You can wire in an external sensor into your ecu to get Boost PSI/Bar readings [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x38 -> RAM 0xf15f]

Manifold Absolute Pressure Scaled. MAP measures the boost pressure at the intake manifold. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x45 -> RAM 0xeee1]

Airflow is calculated by maf volts divided by rpm. this value is calculated inside the ecu with a couple of other temperature and barometer variables to give an accurate airflow reading. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x00 -> RAM 0xf0f6, RequestID2 0x01 -> RAM 0xf0f7]
This is a development mod, [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x40 -> RAM 0xeed7]

This is the raw knock sensor voltage as seen by the vehicle ecu. Also see Knock Sum for a more useful and user friendly item to log for determining knock. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x30 -> RAM 0xf15d]

DataItem RequestID 72 contains some bit flags for knock [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x72 -> RAM 0xf201]

Evo7/8 [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x6A -> RAM 0xf3a9] [SESSION 5 2026-07: value CONFIRMED semantically correct - direct per-cylinder ADC-ch6-derived knock intensity written by knock_process_main (0x2933b), scaled via mul_fixedpoint_8, clamped 0-255. See logging.txt session 5.]

Evo7/8 [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x6B -> RAM 0xf967] [SESSION 5 2026-07: address CONFIRMED, but value is a snapshot of the PREVIOUS cycle's 0xF3AA (clamped per-cylinder retard output) taken by knock_process_main, not an independent 'base' reading. See logging.txt session 5.]

Evo7/8 [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x6C -> RAM 0xf963] [SESSION 5 2026-07: address CONFIRMED, but value is the FINAL per-cylinder knock decision/trigger magnitude after knock_process_main's full threshold/hysteresis chain - a derived decision value, not raw ADC variance. See logging.txt session 5.]

Evo7/8 [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x6D -> RAM 0xf969] [SESSION 5 2026-07: value CONFIRMED semantically correct - delta between current and previous 0xF3A8 knock-ADC reading, computed by knock_process_main (or forced 0 under a gating flag). See logging.txt session 5.]

Evo7/8 [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x6E -> RAM 0xf3b7] [SESSION 5 2026-07: value CONFIRMED - persistence/duration counter written by knock_process_main, increments while clean, resets and sets a lockup flag (RAM 0xF200 bit0) under sustained knock. See logging.txt session 5.]

Evo7/8 [RAM address arithmetic CONFIRMED: RequestID 0x6F -> RAM 0xefc3, verified via adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0.] [SESSION 5 2026-07: SEMANTIC LABEL REFUTED - this is NOT a knock flag on this ECU. Full decompile of knock_process_main (0x2933b) confirms it never touches RAM 0xEFC2/EFC3. The actual writers (FUN_000252f9 and tcu_rx_main_scheduler) are throttle/crank-transient delta detectors, both writing ROM constant 0xB24, unrelated to knock sum or knock ADC. tcu_rx_main_scheduler reads it back to select between two TCU thresholds - consistent with a shared 'large throttle movement' mode flag. Treat this item as MISLABELED, not merely unconfirmed. See logging.txt session 5 for full trace.]

ActuatorListItem.Notes: Forces the fuel pump on for testing. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x01 -> RAM 0xf0f7]
Cycles the EGR solenoid. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x02 -> RAM 0xf0fd]
Cycles the Evap Purge solenoid. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x03 -> RAM 0xf0fc]
Cycles the A/C clutch relay. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x04 -> RAM 0xf343]
Forces radiator/condenser fan to high speed. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x05 -> RAM 0xf363]
Forces radiator/condenser fan to low speed. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x06 -> RAM 0xf361]

Locks timing to 5 degrees base for syncing physical distributor/cam angle sensor. [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x17 -> RAM 0xf13d]

Locks ISC stepper motor position to mid-way to adjust base idle screw (BISS). [RAM CONFIRMED via Ghidra decompilation of adc_sensor_convert_single (0x171c3) + MUT Table @ ROM 0x2fad0, verified 2026-07: RequestID 0x1A -> RAM 0xf1df]
